name: song-builder-buffer
note: M-13 g2 (BUFFER PERSISTENCE + REORDER): the section buffer must SURVIVE a reload and its rows must be REORDERABLE. Persona at 412x915: build C-F-G, add as Verse; build Am, add as Chorus; assert 2 sections + the additive '<storagePrefix>.builderBuffer.v1' key holds them in order (the live app mounts with storagePrefix='roadcase-'+profile.id, not the bare 'songbook.' default the unit tests use - the scenario resolves the real key at runtime rather than hardcoding a prefix). Reload the page (same origin, same context - localStorage persists) WITHOUT ever revisiting Compose first, then confirm the tray restores both chips from the persisted key alone (mount()'s initial renderProg() must do this, not a tab-visit trigger). Tap the second chip's up-reorder handle -> Chorus moves before Verse, both on-screen AND in the persisted key. Assemble and confirm the built song's section order reflects the REORDER (Chorus, then Verse) and the buffer clears. Red-first: before this goalpost, the tray started empty on every reload (no builderBuffer key existed) and no .songSectMove buttons existed at all.

steps.label: one buffered section (Verse) before the second is added
set the second section label to Chorus
two buffered sections, order Verse then Chorus
the additive *.builderBuffer.v1 key exists and persists both sections in order BEFORE any reload (throws, not a truthy-string escape, when the key is missing)
steps.js: (function(){var bufKey=Object.keys(localStorage).filter(function(k){return /\.builderBuffer\.v1$/.test(k)})[0];if(!bufKey)throw new Error('no *.builderBuffer.v1 key in localStorage');var a=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(bufKey));return a.length===2 && a[0].label==='Verse' && a[1].label==='Chorus'})()

steps.label: after a full reload (never revisiting Compose), the buffer key still holds both sections - proves persistence is real storage, not in-memory state
steps.js: (function(){var bufKey=Object.keys(localStorage).filter(function(k){return /\.builderBuffer\.v1$/.test(k)})[0];if(!bufKey)throw new Error('buffer key vanished across reload');var a=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(bufKey));return a.length===2})()

steps.label: mount()'s initial render restores the tray chips from the persisted key alone (DOM present even before revisiting the Compose tab)
the up handle swapped Chorus before Verse on-screen
the reorder persisted to the builderBuffer key immediately
steps.js: (function(){var bufKey=Object.keys(localStorage).filter(function(k){return /\.builderBuffer\.v1$/.test(k)})[0];if(!bufKey)throw new Error('buffer key missing after reorder');var a=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(bufKey));return a.length===2 && a[0].label==='Chorus' && a[1].label==='Verse'})()

steps.label: the first chip's up handle is now disabled (buffer start)
the last chip's down handle is disabled (buffer end)
the assembled song's section headers reflect the REORDER - Chorus before Verse
buffer + its persisted key both clear after assemble
